programme.title: Forgotten on Sinjar: A Story of Survival
programme.desc: Emma Broyan, a German Yazidi activist, moves to Greece to help survivors of the Yazidi genocide; Emma's experience exposes the horror committed on the Yazidi people at the hands of terrorists, while the world watched it all unfold on television.

programme.title: Everest Dark

programme.title: Terry Venables: A Man Can Dream
programme.desc: A look at the life and times of the football player-turned-manager, who died in November 2023, intertwined with the story of England's unforgettable run at Euro 96, with insight from players, friends and family.

programme.title: QPR: The Four Year Plan
programme.desc: An insight into the running of Queens Park Rangers football club, as cameras were given unprecedented access to the boardroom and the first team. In 2007, the club was facing relegation from the Championship as well as financial ruin, before being bought by F1 supremo Bernie Ecclestone and Italian businessman Flavio Briatore. Soon after, they were joined on the board by Indian steel magnate Lakshm

programme.title: Adam Hills: Foot Fault
programme.desc: The comedian and host of The Last Leg takes viewers behind the scenes of a global campaign to promote para-standing tennis and its potential as a future Grand Slam and Paralympic event. Adam meets and plays with former Grand Slam winners and uncovers the barriers faced by aspiring tennis players with disabilities who want to be included by their sport at the highest level.

programme.title: Discovering: Meg Ryan
programme.desc: A profile of the American actress, who starred in When Harry Met Sally, Sleepless in Seattle, You've Got Mail and Courage Under Fire.

programme.title: Discovering: Brad Pitt
programme.desc: A profile of the American actor, whose films include Thelma & Louise, Seven, Fight Club and 12 Monkeys, and won an Oscar for his role in Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.

programme.title: Discovering: Matt Damon
programme.desc: The life and career of the American actor, who won an Oscar for the screenplay of Good Will Hunting and starred in The Martian and the Bourne franchise.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of director Christopher Nolan, whose films include Memento, Insomnia, the Dark Knight trilogy, The Prestige, Inception, Interstellar, Dunkirk and Tenet.

programme.title: The Loneliest Whale: The Search for 52
programme.desc: Documentary about the search to find a rare whale that calls out at a frequency unrecognised by other whales. This mysterious creature is believed to have lived its life in complete solitude, pointing to a greater awareness of human loneliness than ever before.

programme.title: I Am Bruce Lee
programme.desc: The life and career of the actor and martial artist widely known for his work in Enter the Dragon and Fist of Fury, offering a perspective on his rise to fame.

programme.title: Benetton Formula
programme.desc: The history of the Formula 1 team led by Alessandro Benetton, told through the memories of its key members and archive footage.

programme.title: Hill
programme.desc: A profile of ex-Formula One driver Damon Hill, revealing how he overcame the adversity of losing his father Graham to follow in his footsteps and become world champion himself.

programme.title: My Icon: Pam Cookey
programme.desc: The former England netball captain discusses the individuals that have inspired her during her life and career.

programme.title: Williams & Mansell: Red 5
programme.desc: Documentary charting the rise of Nigel Mansell, the Williams F1 racing team and how they came together to fight for the Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship.

programme.title: My Icon: Linford Christie
programme.desc: The former sprint superstar and Olympic gold medalist discusses the individuals that have inspired him in his life and career.

programme.title: Busby
programme.desc: A profile of former Manchester United manager Matt Busby, who transformed football through a career of great talent and tragedy. Featuring previously unseen archive footage and interviews with those who knew the man best.

programme.title: Germanwings: What Happened on Flight 9525?
programme.desc: The circumstances surrounding the crash of an Airbus A320-211 in 2015, which killed 150 people and was found to have been deliberately caused by the co-pilot.

programme.title: Bowling for Columbine
programme.desc: Michael Moore's Oscar-winning documentary exploring America's love affair with firearms. Going beyond public opinion, the film-maker examines corporate and political influence, visiting a bank where weapons are given to customers, as well as talking to Charlton Heston, one of the most famous proponents of every American's right to bear arms.

programme.title: Forced Out
programme.desc: The stories of men and women dishonourably discharged from the Army, Navy and RAF because of their sexuality, with many imprisoned, having medals removed or losing their service pensions.

programme.title: The Ibiza Affair: Uncovered
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the rise and fall of Heinz-Christian Strache and the role journalism plays in controlling those in power.

programme.title: Discovering: Frances McDormand
programme.desc: The life and career of the Academy Award-winning actress famed for her performances in Fargo and more.

programme.title: Discovering: Meg Ryan
programme.desc: A profile of the American actress, who starred in When Harry Met Sally, Sleepless in Seattle, You've Got Mail and Courage Under Fire.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of writer and film-maker Nora Ephron, who was nominated for Academy Awards for her screenplays for Silkwood, When Harry Met Sally and Sleepless in Seattle.

programme.title: MoviePass, MovieCrash
programme.desc: The story of MoviePass, the company that offered cinema tickets at a discounted rate. It was a movie lover's dreaam, but in just eight years it went from being the fastest-growing subscription service since Spotify to total bankruptcy. This documentary chronicles the visionary mission of its co-founders, its early success and how it finally failed due to mismanagement and corporate greed.

programme.title: Rise of the Superheroes
programme.desc: A look at how the tales of comic book superheroes like Batman and the Avengers were adapted for cinema before going on to dominate Hollywood and captivate audiences worldwide.

programme.title: Jackie Stewart
programme.desc: Documentary about Formula 1 great Jackie Stewart, from his humble beginnings outside Glasgow to winning three World Drivers' Championships.

programme.title: Everton: Howard's Way
programme.desc: Documentary film telling the story of Everton and how they reached their their trophy-winning peak in the 1980s under the stewardship of Howard Kendall.

programme.title: Lancaster
programme.desc: The story of the legendary bomber synonymous with the Dambusters, the night raids against Germany and the brave men and women who made them possible.

programme.title: Bobby Robson: More Than a Manager
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the career of the much-loved former England, Barcelona and Newcastle United manager. Via the Hand of God and Paul Gascoigne's tears, to titles across Europe, Robson overcame the most extreme challenges - including fighting for his life after being diagnosed with cancer - before returning to football to manage his beloved Newcastle.

programme.title: Blur: To the End
programme.desc: Documentary tracing the relationship of the band's Damon Albarn, Graham Coxon, Alex James and Dave Rowntree over the course of three decades, unfolding against the backdrop of the quartet collaborating on 10 new songs ahead of two sold-out concerts at Wembley Stadium.

programme.title: Jealous Guy: The Assassination of John Lennon
programme.desc: An exploration of the life and career of the Beatles superstar, with a look at the strange parallels between him and his killer Mark David Chapman.

programme.title: Dave Not Coming Back
programme.desc: The story of two divers who embarked on a long and dangerous mission to retrieve a dead body from a freshwater cave in South Africa, with devastating consequences.

programme.title: Secrets of Wild Australia
programme.desc: The marsupial family, which ranges from the iconic kangaroo to the cheerful quokka. While they come in all shapes and sizes, there are many similarities.

programme.title: New Kids in the Wild
programme.desc: In India, baby lion-tailed macaque Ravi faces life in a shrinking habitat. Luckily, he has a caring family watching over him.

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Documentary about a group of elusive chimpanzees living in the Fongoli region of Senegal, on the southern edge of the Sahara Desert. It's the hottest, driest place in the world that chimps can live and they really shouldn't be here - so how do they survive?

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: As vet Phil handles some prickly patients, rescuer Chantel extracts a sooty possum duo from a chimney and vet Meaghan unhooks the catch of the day.

programme.title: Guardians of the Wild
programme.desc: A look at how the lifeless Liuwa Plains National Park, having been ravaged by poachers, is now being restored to its former glory by a remarkable team of conservationists.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Everyone is on edge as the team reunite reluctant mother Dilla with her new baby Delilah. Clara and Clarita go on their final journey.

programme.title: My Best Friend's an Animal
programme.desc: A Tasmanian devil shows its softer side and Buttons the deer enjoys a family dinner. Spud the 800-kilogram bull plays a game of football and a sea turtle makes some big moves.

programme.title: My Best Friend's an Animal
programme.desc: Dingani the baboon shares a one-of-a-kind kinship with his primate pal Lucy and a pig celebrates the anniversary of its rescue. Banksi the fruit bat has a flap around.

programme.title: The Wild Wild East
programme.desc: The wildlife of the Maritime Provinces on Canada's eastern shore. The first edition visits Prince Edward Island to explore the family life of the red fox, sly creatures adept at living just on the fringes of society.

programme.title: The Hungry Games: Alaska Big Bear Challenge
programme.desc: A look at how bears of Alaska's Katmai National Park bulk up for hibernation over 150 days, battling the elements and each other.

programme.title: Shark Squad
programme.desc: Leading shark ecologist Mauricio Hoyos attempts a TV first as he attaches a camera to the dorsal fin of a great white shark off Guadalupe Island.

programme.title: Pridelands: Wilderness Reborn
programme.desc: Following an Australian family as they leave their comfortable life behind and set out to re-habitate a South African private hunting ranch called Pridelands.

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Documentary about a group of elusive chimpanzees living in the Fongoli region of Senegal, on the southern edge of the Sahara Desert. It's the hottest, driest place in the world that chimps can live and they really shouldn't be here - so how do they survive?

programme.title: Wolves and Lions: Old Enemies
programme.desc: A look at the strengths, social strategies and hierarchies behind how lions and wolves in Afirca raise and protect their young.

programme.title: Macro Worlds
programme.desc: Documentary revealing that when winter comes, many insects have different strategies to see them through - even if they can only ensure that their offspring will live on.

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part documentary, the naturalist explores the origins of life on Earth. Beginning the journey near his childhood home in Charnwood Forest, Leicestershire, he learns how a fossil discovery transformed scientists' understanding of the evolution of complex organisms, and in Newfoundland and Australia he reveals the earliest kinds of animal to exist on the planet. With the help o

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part documentary, the naturalist travels to Burgess Shale in Canada's Rocky Mountains, where he explores how evolution gained pace once creatures developed mouths and mobility. In Scotland, he reveals that one of the smallest fossils found holds the secret to how animals began surviving on dry land, and explains the bloom of life during the oxygen-rich Carboniferous pe

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part documentary, the naturalist explores the origins of life on Earth. Beginning the journey near his childhood home in Charnwood Forest, Leicestershire, he learns how a fossil discovery transformed scientists' understanding of the evolution of complex organisms, and in Newfoundland and Australia he reveals the earliest kinds of animal to exist on the planet. programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part documentary, the naturalist travels to Burgess Shale in Canada's Rocky Mountains, where he explores how evolution gained pace once creatures developed mouths and mobility. In Scotland, he reveals that one of the smallest fossils found holds the secret to how animals began surviving on dry land, and explains the bloom of life during the oxygen-rich Carboniferous pe

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A further selection of creepy-crawly close-ups, focusing on courtship in the bug world. Highlights include scorpions shaking their booty, the perfume-wearing nasonia wasp and the tropical cricket, for whom romance goes out of the window as it uses bribery to seal the deal with would-be partners. One potential partner to steer well clear of, however, is the praying mantis, the female of which is no

programme.title: Pride Rules
programme.desc: The challenges that lions face during the rainy season, when everything turns green and spoils the big cats' camouflage.

programme.title: Predators
programme.desc: Tom Hardy presents a journey to the harsh Okavango Delta as the Matata pride do battle with wildfires and flooding in a high-stakes fight to keep their cubs alive.

programme.title: Wild Latin America
programme.desc: A newborn whale draws its first breath and then preparation begins for one of the longest migrations on the planet.

programme.title: Wild Latin America
programme.desc: Tensions rise as breeding season nears for the squirrel monkeys. With enemies closing in, dominant female Alba must find her alpha male suitor.

programme.title: Pride Rules
programme.desc: The challenges that lions face during the rainy season, when everything turns green and spoils the big cats' camouflage.

programme.title: Predators
programme.desc: Tom Hardy presents a journey to the harsh Okavango Delta as the Matata pride do battle with wildfires and flooding in a high-stakes fight to keep their cubs alive.

programme.title: Boss Shark
programme.desc: Specialists study a school of great whites in the waters off New Zealand's Stewart Island in the hope of finding an elusive and intimidating alpha shark.

programme.title: Life's Journey
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how Africa's creatures overcome danger when they are at their most vulnerable. For the first few weeks of life, the world is a frightening place. Danger lurks around every corner and animals cannot afford to let their guard down.

programme.title: Life's Journey
programme.desc: The obstacles young animals have to overcome on their way to adulthood, when their chances of survival increase hugely.

programme.title: Animal Empires
programme.desc: Raising the next generation is the number one priority for every animal species. For some creatures, an entire village lends a helping hand, but others prefer to go solo.

programme.title: Animal Empires
programme.desc: The threat of extinction is all too real in the wild, but even against terrible odds, many animals fight back from the brink to continue their empires.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: The powers of fire and ice collide in Iceland, actually Earth's largest volcanic island. Take a journey to this country driven by the forces of nature.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: A return to Iceland to see how its landscapes are changing through the retreat of its glaciers. Every year, their tremendous power alters the nation for ever.

programme.title: Arctic from Above
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin travels to one of Earth's last great wildernesses to reveal fresh insights about its wildlife, including polar bears, narwhals, wolves, reindeer, leopard seals and killer whales.

programme.title: Snow Leopards with Dan O'Neill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Biologist and naturalist Dan O'Neill sets out on a mission to protect one of Earth's most elusive creatures - the snow leopard.

programme.title: Snow Leopards with Dan O'Neill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Navigate the harsh Mongolian wilderness with Dan as he searches for the largest snow leopard ever recorded, known as 'the Dude'.

programme.title: Wild Castles
programme.desc: In Slovenia lies the world's largest castle that is carved into a cave - a subterranean haven for the region's birds, boars and mountain goats.

programme.title: Land of Primates
programme.desc: From monkeys to lemurs and apes to human beings, primates are unique mammals who have taken an extraordinary evolutionary leap. See how they put their brains to work. Millions of years ago, animals were stranded on the shores of Madagascar, leading to remarkable adaptations. A case in point - the ring-tailed lemur.

programme.title: Macaque Island
programme.desc: Exploring the lives of two troops of long-tailed macaques residing in the forests of Singapore, revealing the fascinating world of these curious and intelligent primates. Matnoor rules over the 30 members of the Street troop, wihile Aarav is the old alpha male of the wilder Hill troop.

programme.title: Macaque Island
programme.desc: The Hill Troop invade the Street Troop's turf, before the Sisterhood plots to kidnap Sara's baby. Meanwhile, alpha Loci discovers his old rival is back.

programme.title: Macaque Island
programme.desc: A brawl erupts after the Street Troop finds the Hill Troop on their turf, before alpha wannabe Ajit makes his move.

programme.title: Wolves and Lions: Old Enemies
programme.desc: A look at how canids and felines deploy an arsenal of hunting skills. Felines face endurance, while wolves rely on their privileged sense of smell in a fight for survival.

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Following his visit to the Great Barrier Reef in 1957, naturalist and broadcaster David Attenborough returns and uses the latest filming techniques to unlock the secrets of the natural wonder. In the first edition, he descends beneath the waves at night and uses cutting-edge technology to generate computer scans of the sea floor. He also learns about a complex community of animals, as well as the

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Using the latest technology, the naturalist explores the shark-infested waters of Osprey Reef in the Triton submersible, before returning to the nesting grounds of the Green Sea Turtle, 60 years after his first visit to Raine Island. He also marvels at manta ray cleaning stations on Lady Elliot Island, and tracks dwarf minke whales to discover their surprising reasons for returning, and why the re

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The naturalist examines what the next few decades may hold for this remarkable ecosystem, and looks at the work of scientists who are trying to preserve the area and the species that inhabit it, assisted by research vessel the Alucia. He also embarks on an ambitious attempt to dive further into the reef than ever before to witness the resilient coral that live down in the depths and see if they ho

programme.title: Woodpeckers: The Hole Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the unique abilities of the woodpecker to reveal what makes this bird so special. Narrated by Paul Giamatti.

programme.title: Wolves and Lions: Old Enemies
programme.desc: A look at how canids and felines deploy an arsenal of hunting skills. Felines face endurance, while wolves rely on their privileged sense of smell in a fight for survival.

programme.title: Beasts of the Big Blue
programme.desc: Exploring New Zealand's marine wildlife, beginning with Snares Island in the southern Pacific and its crested penguins, petrels and sea lions.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they make George Washington's favourite tipple? How do they crash test aircraft seats without crashing a plane? How do they produce billions of batteries a year.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they mine nickel to turn into nickels? How do they build the lock gates for canals to allow barges to climb hills? How do they put the bubbles in bubble tea.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike wants a high performance car with an historic badge, but it's hard to find a Saab 9-3 Turbo at a good price. With tight margins will Edd have any room for manoeuvre.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike heads to America in search of one of the finest cars in the US, the Dodge Charger. But such a prestigious car carries a hefty price tag. Can he make a profit.

programme.title: Shed And Buried
programme.desc: In Lancashire, Henry and Sam explore a garage packed with hundreds of treasures and trinkets. They encounter various military vehicles and a lovely old petrol pump.

programme.title: Shed And Buried
programme.desc: Henry and Sam are across the sea in Northern Ireland, searching for collections they've never seen before. Will they find any unusual or unique items.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: Toothpicks, acrylic bathtubs, helicopters and beer. More objects are put under the microscope as we find out how they were made.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: Nails, staples, safety glasses, fabrics and bicycles are put under the microscope to find out how they were made.

programme.title: Chasing Classic Cars
programme.desc: Wayne gets a lead on a cache of three 308 Ferraris in a nearby garage. Then, Roger works on a belt job while Wayne puts on his sales hat to drum up clients.

programme.title: Chasing Classic Cars
programme.desc: Wayne gets a lead on a collection of his newest obsession, Brass cars. There's a 1913 Hudson, a 1910 Stoddard-Dayton, a 1906 Oldsmobile and a Redding motorcycle.

programme.title: Chasing Classic Cars
programme.desc: Wayne helps Olympic gold medallist Jonny Moseley and the Squaw Valley ski resort find a vehicle that could have been at the 1960 Winter Olympics.

programme.title: Chasing Classic Cars
programme.desc: Wayne finds a rare 1934 Lincoln KB Convertible. But to buy it, he must meet a high price and buy two other cars - a '70s Chrysler K Car and '60s Buick convertible.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike sources a VW 181 Thing from a collector, but is forced to dig deep for the purchase, meaning there isn't much left in the kitty for Edd to refurbish it.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike is ecstatic when he snaps up a cult VW Corrado coupe with only one previous owner for a bargain price. But with cheap cars come expensive surprises..

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike wants to splash the cash on a custom bus but Edd favours a more conservative campervan. Edd is an air-cooled expert but will this strategy pay dividends.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Part 1. Auto expert Mike Brewer and mechanical maestro Edd China get under the bonnet of a VW Beetle, but will they be able to breathe life into this ancient wonder.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Part 2. Mechanical duo Mike and Edd are working on a VW Beetle which begins to take shape. But it's not all smooth cruising for the team in this topsy-turvy restoration.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike hunts out a classic camper but impossibly high prices force him to settle for a less popular panel model. Can Edd realise the ambitious plans for its upgrade.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike finds a 1970 VW Flashback that needs some love and attention from Ant to be roadworthy again. If they can finish the upgrade on time, will they make a profit.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike finds a 1992 Corrado G60 smothered in aftermarket mods. Parts are rare and superchargers can be costly, but Elvis determines to get this risky project done.

programme.title: Shed And Buried: Classic Cars
programme.desc: In Northamptonshire, Henry and Fuzz dive into an eclectic mix of British and American vehicles. Henry and Fuzz end up smitten with a customised Hot Rod.

programme.title: Shed And Buried: Classic Cars
programme.desc: Henry and Fuzz rummage through two huge sheds of vintage military vehicles. There are army quad bikes, amphibious troop carriers, and a Ferret Scout car.

programme.title: Shed And Buried: Classic Cars
programme.desc: Henry and Fuzz stop by a shed in Leighton Buzzard filled with astonishing American muscle cars. The pair pick a trusty classic with room for a profit.

programme.title: Shed And Buried: Classic Cars
programme.desc: Henry and Fuzz head up to Yorkshire, hoping to revisit 'the one that got away'. It's a rare E-type Jaguar Rat Rod with a huge surprise under the bonnet.

programme.title: Richard Hammond's Workshop
programme.desc: New staff are thrown in the deep end on a rare Lotus, and a unique race car. Richard's beloved steam-car restoration threatens to run out of puff.

programme.title: Shed And Buried: Classic Cars
